Renewable energy plays a key role in the transition to a sustainable energy system and is essential for mitigating climate change.

Energy from polluting renewable sources has the same problem as energy produced by fossil fuels: in combustion they emit carbon dioxide, greenhouse gas, and are often even more polluting, since combustion is not so clean, emitting soots and other solid particles.

A major development of wind power can therefore be one of the most effective measures to avoid the greenhouse effect, since, globally, the electricity sector is considered to be responsible for 29% of the global CO2 emissions. As a clean energy it is, it contributes to minimizing global warming.
Since certain renewable energy sources provide relatively low energy intensity, distributed over large areas, new types of "central" are needed to convert them into usable sources.
